mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, forces the Federal Reserve to maintain a restrictive policy stance. A high FEDFUNDS rate increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Jan 2023, CPI YoY is ~6.5% and FEDFUNDS is 4.33%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a 10-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: A strong and improving labor market, proxied by a falling unemployment rate, signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings power. This fundamental strength supports higher equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.4% in Jan 2023 is a 50-year low, indicating labor market tightness.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in UNRATE exhibits a consistently positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and residential investment (HOUST) provides a powerful, joint signal of a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand. This combination foreshadows declining corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, positive surprises in industrial production indicate that supply-side constraints are easing. This alleviates input cost pressures for firms, improving profit margins and leading to positive equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-pandemic recovery has been characterized by supply chain disruptions.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) during periods of high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is already tight (low UNRATE), an unexpectedly large increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) signals an overheating economy. This forces the central bank to adopt a more hawkish stance, increasing the probability of aggressive rate hikes which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Jan 2023 jobs report showed a 517k gain with UNRATE at 3.4%.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the Federal Funds rate is a primary component of the risk-free rate used to discount future equity cash flows. A higher FEDFUNDS rate directly compresses equity valuations by increasing this discount rate, leading to negative pressure on the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: doc:Standard discounted cash flow (DCF) valuation models
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating real economic growth (GDPC1) and persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment. This regime is uniquely damaging for corporate profits, as costs rise while demand falters, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth is lower than prior quarter's growth AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 5%
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive during quarters that meet the trigger condition over a 20-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly payrolls report (PAYEMS) provides a timely signal of economic resilience. This boosts near-term investor sentiment and reduces immediate recession fears, causing a positive short-term reaction in the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:Market commentary frequently cites jobs data as a key short-term driver.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a top-quintile monthly PAYEMS print is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.
